sections.paragraph:
prop. subst. addition, accumulation; hence part. indicating accession, like אַף (which see).-

(1) also, Gen 3:6, 22 7:3 19:21 , 35 Gen 19:35 30:15 35:17 , etc. It is prefixed to the words to which it refers, like the Lat. etiam; but when words are repeated for the sake of emphasis, it is put between them; as with pronouns, הִיא גַּם הִיא she herself also, Gen 20:5 בְּפִיו גַּם הוּא in his mouth also, 2Sa 17:5 Pro 23:15 Gen 27:34 (comp. Gram. § 92, 1; [119, 4]; Lehrg. § 191); used with verbs, Gen 46:4, וְאָנֹכִי אַעַלְךָ גַם עָלֹה “and I will also come up with thee.” Gen 31:15 1Sa 1:6. It is sometimes put at the beginning of a sentence, referring not to the nearest word, but to one more remote. Gen 16:13, הֲגַם הֲלֹם רָאִיתִי אַחֲרֵי רֹאִי “do I here see (i.e. live) also after the vision (of God)?” Pro 19:2 20:11 Isa 30:33. (Observe the same thing of the part. אַךְ Isa 34:14 רַק Isa 28:19.) Poet. used sometimes for the simple וְ (compare Dan. og and); Joe 1:12 Jud. 5:4 Psa 137:1. גַּם … גַּם also … also, both, and, Gen 24:25 43:8 Exo 12:31 that … so, Jer 51:12 and even put three times, Isa 48:8 גַּם … וְגַם Gen 24:44.

(2) Not unfrequently it is used as an intensitive, even (see אַף No. 1 ). Pro 14:20 17:26 Joe 3:2 with a particle of negation, not even, Psal. 14:3 53:4 2Sa 17:12, 13 2Sa 17:13(comp. עַד No. 3, a).

(3) It often only serves to make a sentence emphatic, and sometimes may be rendered yea, indeed, truly, or else it shews that the next word takes a considerable emphasis. Job 18:5, גַּם אֹור רְשָׁעִים יִדְעָךְ “yea, the light of the wicked shall be put out.” 1Sa 24:12, רְאֵה גַּם רְאֵה see, yea, see (fiehe, fiehe doch!). Gen 29:30, “and he loved Rachel more than Leah.” Job 2:10, “shall we receive good from the Lord, and shall we not,” etc. Hos 9:12, “for woe to them!” Gen 42:22 Job 13:16 16:19 Isa 66:4. So גַּם שְׁנַיִם alle bende, both together, Gen 27:45 Pro 17:15 20:10 , 12 Pro 20:12 1Sa 4:17, גַּם כֹּל all together, alle zufammen; 2Sa 19:31 Psa 25:3. גַּם עַתָּה Germ. nun gut, Gen 44:10.

(4) גַּם־כִּי even if, followed by a fut. Isa 1:15 Hos 8:10 9:16 and without כִּי Isa 49:15 Psa 95:9. There also occurs כִּי גַם Ecc 4:14 8:12 .

(5) advers. however, but, chiefly followed by a negative part. Psa 129:2 Ecc 4:16 Eze 16:28.
